summary: Messenger library silently re-uploads incoming photo URLs to author-controlled ImgBB account
details: The package advertises itself as a Facebook Messenger client library. When an installer uses the documented event listeners api.listen / api.listenE2EE, src/listenMqtt.js:63-69 invokes attachImageUrlToAttachment, which calls api._imgUpload on every incoming photo attachment URL. That helper (src/uploadImageToImgbb.js) POSTs the URL to https://api.imgbb.com/1/upload with a hardcoded API key (IMGBB_KEY = "3e198e6ffe205d1c7968a92fd92177c9") tied to the package author's ImgBB account. ImgBB then fetches and stores the referenced image under the author's account. The README only documents uploadImageToImgbb as an explicit manual helper; the automatic re-hosting of every received photo (including private 1:1 direct-message images) on the listener path is undisclosed. Any caller using the advertised listener API silently leaks photo content from their Messenger conversations to a third-party image host under the author's control — the silent-relay shape: hardcoded destination, caller-supplied data, undocumented routing triggered by normal use of the advertised API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
